Off the books, certain agencies found the data useful, and looked the other way.” He sighed. “I only learned later that the funding for the project was not entirely above board. We could not tell the authorities about all the subjects. The lawsuits would cripple us. We decided that anyone showing symptoms would be locked away if we could arrange it, but that too risked exposure. Eventually we started putting them down. I only agreed in the most extreme of cases at first, but then once I was in, I could not get out. I could both continue my work and get my hands dirty, or I would be put down too." The doctor turned white at the thought.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash let out a sigh, and wiped his face.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Which brings me to you. I just cannot do it. You are a good man, and I just couldn't do it. Then you had to show up here and ruin my one good deed. But it occurs to me that we may be able to help each other now. There is another volunteer that was showing promise, and then he turned out to be the worst of them all. He is out there in the world causing untold harm and havoc, and he must be stopped. However, despite my best resources, I cannot find him. It's possible that you, with your Special Forces background, could." <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Alright, let's just say that I'm buying your story for now. Something still doesn't add up. I was not released from some clean room. I went missing from a top secret training facility, and weeks later I was found on a desert island with no memory. How does that fit in to your volunteer story?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"You were special, you and your friend. We needed subjects from specific backgrounds that were not as likely to volunteer. So, we arranged to have them delivered. I cannot tell you more, I wish I could. I was the Chief Scientist, but I did not handle the... Business End. You showed up and I put you into the clean room before you woke. Afterward, you were delivered back to your people. I cannot tell you who, or how...” Doctor Briar leaned closer, "But I can tell you that we could not have done it without help from your own government. You be careful who you trust. I know DARPA funds were siphoned more than once for our project." <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash sat, silently. Staring into the floor. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The Doctor walked around to his desk, and pulled open the drawer. The doctor came back around, and dropped a small white envelope into Ash's inside jacket pocket, then put his finger to his mouth. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"I don't believe there is anything else I can tell you at the moment. I cannot help you escape from here without looking complicit. The system will be reset shortly. When it does the cameras will reboot. There is an exit down the hall that leads to the outside."<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He pressed a button on the wall behind him.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“Yes Doctor Briar."&nbsp; A man answered. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"We are done in here, there is nothing more I can learn today. Take him to his cell, and we can do this again tomorrow." Dr. Briar smiled and winked, then his eyes turned downward.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Two guards entered the room, and asked him to stand up. They recuffed him, and motioned to the door. As they passed the desk he shoved his weight into one guard knocking him to the ground. The other was blinded by a quick head butt to the bridge of the nose. Ash put his shoulder into the Doctor's chest, and kicked his knee to the side. The Doctor went down to the ground.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"A little too easily" Ash thought, "but hey, this isn't the Oscars". <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He grabbed the keys from the desk and ran down the hall, unlocking his cuffs on the way.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash crashed through the door, and headed off into the night. The system must have eventually reset, because he could hear sirens in the distance, but only after he was a few miles from the facility. With no camera's it was a wild goose chase to know which direction he had headed. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Recovered<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta got the call from Chester that Ash had escaped out of the back entrance. One emergency powered camera showed him heading east toward town. Which left only three directions he may have really been headed. She glanced at the Google map of the area, in satellite view. She had been here only last night. But the terrain looked different in the early morning light. North and West were both fields and highways. South led back to the beach. Beaches are easier to get lost in. He could take the conspicuous clothing off without needing to find an entirely new outfit. He may have to grab a pair of shorts. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta drove the newly borrowed Corvette down to the coast. She saw a bum sitting on the bench with what appeared to be a newly acquired military grade jacket. She pulled up to him, and asked where he got it. Then he pointed her toward the beach shop nearby.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Thanks buddy." Eta nodded and pulled up along the curb in front of the shop. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Took you long enough." A voice spoke from behind her. She could feel her heart jump into her throat. How does he do that? <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"You could get shot..." She said without turning around. "... For sneaking up on a girl like that."<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"I could" Ash sat came up and leaned on the window of the convertible she’d ‘borrowed’, "but life is worth the risk some times."<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"So..." Eta pulled her sun glasses down. He was wearing the same pants from the night before, but he had torn them into cut-off shorts and he managed to acquire a new a new tank top that said “Malibu Style”. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He had put on a few pounds since they worked together in Israel, but he didn't age badly. She cast the thought aside. No. Not him. "Now what grandpa?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Now, we make sure I wasn't followed. Then I take this" Ash pulled a thumb drive out of a white envelope. "to Chester." He slipped it back into the envelope, then his pocket. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“The audio was out thanks to Chester’s virus and the system reboot. But it kept his access to the cameras, even though there’s was down. Chester noticed that he put something into your pocket before you escaped. What’s on it? And why did he give it to you?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“I didn’t exactly escape. The good Doctor Briar let me go… well… I guess he didn’t let me go, as much as he didn’t make it hard to escape. The only thing in here was the thumb drive, and a name on the outside. "Darkmatter". <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“That’s an interesting turn of events.” Eta said. “Why, exactly, would he do that?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“It’s a long story, I’m not sure that I trust him… tell you on the way.” Ash jumped over the door into the passenger seat. “Uh… that is as soon as you tell me where you got a bright blue 2016 Corvette.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta could feel the grin crawl across her face. She couldn’t help it. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;“A bit conspicuous… don’t you think?” Ash turned back to Eta. He had his superior officer face on, but she could see the smile he was fighting back. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“Zillow is amazing for finding wealthy people selling their third homes in this recession. This guy still had his car collection in the garage in one of the photos, and his ‘sophisticated’ key box wasn’t that sophisticated.” She shrugged. “I had to do something with my time while I was waiting on Chester to tell me where to go.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;“Hey” Ash interrupted her moment. Trade spots with me “I’m driving.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">“HA!” Eta snorted. “Go steal… er… borrow… your own sports car. I’m driving.” She looked him in the eye. “Besides, we both know that I’m a better driver, and I’d like to return this in one piece so he never knows it was missing.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash tilted his head and looked like he wanted to say something, then threw a glance at his shoulder stitches and put on his seat belt.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Her fingers fumbled for the phone in her Cowboy Holster bag. She paid UrbanTool $115.00 for this glorified fanny pack. But she hated purses, and this was just that cool. She handed a phone to Ash, “This one is yours, call Chester and let him know I found you.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Then she pressed the start button and the engine roared to life.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Thumb Drive<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: He learned some of the secret to their past, in exchange he is let go to hunt down a bigger fish. Dark Matter. Ash escapes with a thumb drive. It contains a list of everything he knows about Dark Matter's operations. <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Ash could feel his stomach gurgling. He couldn’t tell if it was mad at him for starving it so long, or grateful for the new supply. They had brought a large order from Thai Banquet on the way to Chester’s, and they were all sitting around the table digesting the events of the past few days.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Ash spent most of dinner recapping the conversation with Dr. Briar, while Chester ran a debugging program on a non-network computer. “We’re not about to just open that thing before we know what Trojan horse is on there.” He’d already walked them through making sure that there was no GPS tracker before he let them get close to the city. The video chat came in very handy, he didn’t have to rely on their descriptions.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“So… You guys were actually part of these experiments.” It was more of a statement, than a question. Chester turned to Ash, “Why don’t you remember being a part of it?”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“If Dr. Briar is to be believed” Ash said, “And there’s no telling yet if he is… The experiments cause the cells of the brain to re-structure, in some cases in dramatic ways. He said that these experiments were forcing the next evolution of mankind. Unfortunately, short-term memory loss is common for most test subjects. The memory loss is worse for some than others.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">&nbsp;“Why would some people experience greater effects than others?” Eta asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“The ones that experienced the greatest changes to the brain’s molecular structure have more changes in the temporary memory area of the brain? He said that some people were more susceptible to the experiment. He thinks that these are the humans who were genetically pre-disposed toward the next evolution of mankind, more advanced already, and his experiment just pushes them along. Kind of like a jump start?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Huh… We’re like… X-Men?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Something like that. ‘Evolved’ is the word he used.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta glanced down at her Styrofoam bowl. “I don’t feel very evolved.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Me neither.” Ash took another bite of Pad Thai. “He said the dreams are latent memories from the experiment itself. Some memories, those with more powerful emotions are more ‘sticky’.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">The laptop on the far end of the table beeped. “Done.” Chester said, and he went over to the keyboard and started typing. After a few moments of silent chewing and watching from Eta and Ash, Chester stopped typing and turned the monitor to them. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“So… what is it?” Eta asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Files. Thousands of files. I'm fairly certain that non of this was on the data I hacked from the Tower. This is top secret stuff you wouldn't keep on a connected server. It looks like there is a file for every experiment they ever ran for 20 years. They are grouped into categories. Danger. No Danger. Evaluating. Missing.” Chester pointed to the file names, “Check this out though. Next to each name is an indicator ‘Alive’, ‘Passed’, or ‘Eliminated’.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“That sounds ominous.” Eta pointed with her chopsticks. “Is my name in there?”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Chester’s fingers flew through some files, “Yeppers. You and Ash are both in here. The date says it was about eight years ago. It may take a while to go through these. Most of the file is just charts and graphs.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Well… that certainly matches with the time period we got ‘Medically Discharged’. General Akbar was so mad." Eta said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Doing his best impression, Ash stuck out his chest: “‘Two of my best agents went missing for weeks, and can’t even tell me where they were? Of all the asinine bull! You had better come up with something better than that for your actions, or I’ll have you court martialed.’ “Ash chuckled, “He was not happy.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">&nbsp;“That is, until we were subjected to every test known to man, and he was certain we were telling the truth. Then he just looked crushed, like someone took his candy away.” Eta feigned a pout. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Well, especially for you.” Ash threw a wadded napkin at her. “You were his poster child. The pilot project. The first woman to be allowed to join the Combat Control. You were ‘paving the way for other women’ he said.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Yeah, then he followed it with ‘… so don’t screw it up Romanakov.’ <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“I’m just glad that I was at Fairchild while you were at Lackland. I heard you gave those boys a hard time.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“I did.” Eta’s eyebrows furrowed, “They deserved it too.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“And then when they called us in to the new tactical unit, only 6 chosen in the whole Air Force. He was so mad when he had to discharge us, after all that.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Let’s not pretend he was sentimental. He was just losing his investment. He had to start over with two new recruits, 46 months lost. That’s what really got to him.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Ash could still feel the heat burning up his neck when he was being questioned by his own commanding officer. How dare he accuse him of lying? Ash was the most dedicated servant the US Air Force had ever had, and he was not going to just sit here while some bureaucrat in a uniform questioned his loyalties. That was what he felt. What he did was what he was trained to do, sit there and take it. Submit to the tests, and prove he was right through the process.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Did you see his face when he had to apologize to us?” Eta laughed.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Ash remembered the joy it brought him when the General apologized for his outburst. “He’s been atoning for that ever since. I assume he still contacts you twice a year for progress updates?” Ash asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“He sure does. Like clockwork. Last time-” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Hey guys…” Chester interrupted. “Did you say General Akbar?”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Yes, that was our commanding officer. Why?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Would that be General George Akbar, USAF? Commanding Officer of the Air Force Special Operations Command?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Yes…” Eta sat forward. He ears pulled back ever so slightly when she was interested. Ash had always found that to be a funny quirk. “Why?”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“He’s in these files too.” Chester said, his voice tone sounded as confused as Ash felt.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“General Akbar was experimented on too?” Ash asked. He could feel his own heart beating in his chest. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Not exactly.” Chester said. “He’s under a separate file labeled ‘Contributors’.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">The three of them crowded around the screen, pouring over the file. “General Akbar was the one who delivered us to Dr. Briar?” Ash wasn’t sure if Eta sounded mad, confused, both?<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Hold on…” Chester said. “There are some intake notes here.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">The screen read:<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Colonel Theron "Ash" Ashland, USAF Spec Ops.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Technical Sergeant Nadine Etaine “Eta” Romanakov, USAF Spec Ops.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Contributed as possible use for USAF. DARPA funding under the Enhanced Soldiers initiative. Copy all test results to DARPA and General Akbar.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Wait…” Eta back up into the couch near the table. Her eyes narrowed, and mouth pursed together, “He knew where we were? He knew and still drilled us like that? Of all the-” Eta cut herself off mumbling to herself in Russian.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Ash wasn’t sure how to feel. Confused, sure. Angry, obviously. Yet, somehow, it felt… right. Like that annoying piece you cannot find on those hidden images games his students were always playing on their phones. They would always get so mad that he could spot it in 10 seconds when they had been looking for hours. But this had been a puzzle he just couldn't piece together until just now.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“It makes sense.” Ash said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta threw him a stare.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“No, it makes sense.” He continued. “I always assumed he checked in on us because he was sorry for our strange departure. Or because we were high value assets and he was hoping we’d eventually remember something useful for his investigation.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“OK?” Eta’s eye's told him she was waiting for the punchline.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“He was trying to see if we’d remembered something, but not for his investigation. He was trying to see if his investment had paid off. He wanted super-soldiers, and instead he got broken soldiers with memory loss and bad dreams. He’s been hoping something would eventually happen.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“That lying horses butt.” Eta sat down on the edge of the couch.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“OK.” Chester piped in. “So… what does that mean for us now? Can we trust this guy? This General? Or how about the Dr. Briar?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“As for Dr. Briar, I think we can all agree he may be useful, but we cannot trust him. As for the General, I was trained to never trust anyone. That’s why I’ve only checked in from random payphones that show a circle around a different neighborhood blocks from here.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“You trusted me pretty quickly.” Chester said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Well, I didn’t have much choice with you.” Ash replied. “We did not really having a plan when we got here. Coming to your rescue made plans for that night harder.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Why did you come to rescue me?” Chester inquired. “You could have let it go, you didn’t know that I was worth saving.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Didn’t have much choice their either. Your friend was very convincing.” Ash said. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“My friend?” Chester asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Yeah, what friend?” Eta joined in.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“That night, when I heard the commotion in the alley. I looked around the corner and saw the group harassing you. Then I saw a flash of light. When it cleared I saw a large man, maybe eight feet tall… with a white robe, like Caesar… standing behind you. He looked at you, smiled at me, motioned his hands to the men, and then vanished. I kind of couldn’t help myself. I rushed in without knowing exactly why. How do you turn down a guy like that?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“You saw an angel behind me?” Chester seamed more amused than surprised. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“I guess that’s what you’d call him. Sure. Fits the description.” Ash said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Well how about that.” Chester said. “Good job Herbert.” He gave a high five to the air behind him.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Herbert?” Eta asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“That’s what I call my Angel.” Chester said.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“You’ve seen your angel too?” She asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“No. But God doesn’t really tell us their names, and we’re not supposed to worship them, or get fixated on them. But I always feel better knowing the people I’m hanging with. So I gave him a name.” Chester said flatly. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“You believe in angels huh?” Eta asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Of course. Don’t you?” Chester asked, his eyes widened. “After all you’ve been through lately, surely the supernatural doesn’t shock you?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“I’m still not sure what to believe about all of this. I’ve just been hoping I wasn’t nuts, and for some rational explanation. It sounds like we’re onto one now, with the whole evolution thing. But angels? I mean, next you’re going to tell me you believe in elves and wizards.”&nbsp; <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Not such a wild assumption on your part, but magic has always been the layman’s explanation for things science hasn’t understood yet.” Chester pulled his Android out and held it up to them. “Imagine you went back in time to the days of Knights and Inquisitions. You show them a smart phone, and play music from it, or a Ted Talk, or a video of a cat attacking a lighted Christmas tree. You think they would ask you for one? Maybe a few would, but most would want to burn you at the stake for the use of magic.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Point taken” Eta said, “But still… angels?”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“You think this world is all there is? We are living on one of several dimensions. Angels and Demons are just created beings, like us, that live on another level, impacting ours. Ash merely saw through the veil for a moment.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“And me?” Eta asked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Assuming that Einstein was right about time and space, and string theory is right about multiple dimensions, time is a dimension, like height, width, and depth. If you were to jump out of the four dimensional box, you would be outside of time. Past, present, and future, would all be tangible to you at the same time, like the three dimensions of a Rubik’s Cube are available to you now. So ‘future visions’ are nothing more than your ability to operate, partially and temporarily, outside of space-time.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“That sounds a bit wild, so how did you come to this conclusion?” Eta seamed still unsure.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Math and Science were pre-requisites for a PhD in Computer Science. This is common topic of interest on the Science Chat Rooms.” Chester shrugged. “Hey, it’s getting late. It could take years to sort through these files manually. Let me run a search algorithm to compare the data in the files for your mystery man "Darkmatter" to the internet news feeds and dark web searches. This may help to bring us to the most relevant information. I used to use that when studying for tests. Came in very handy. Who wants to read worthless stuff, when you can jump to the answers? Then we can sort through that in the morning.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta nodded in agreement, and with a yawn headed off down the hall. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Ash piled the plates and such on the counter and put the left-overs in the fridge. “Thanks Chester, for everything. I know we’re imposing here.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Are you kidding?” Chester said. “This is the most fun I’ve had in my life. See you tomorrow.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Observation<o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Dr. Briar pulled up his monitor at his desk in the tower.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Status Report?<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Subject WildCard took the bait.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Good. Keep watch and monitor results.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He pulled out up the footage of Chester's house. The heat signatures showed three inside. Now they would lead him to the real prize.</span><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;"><br clear="all" style="mso-special-character: line-break; page-break-before: always;" /></span> <br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 16.0pt;">Episode 2 - The Parade</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">New Scene 6</span></b><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 16.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Waqas</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">The costumed men and women were dancing through the streets. A Snoopy Balloon passed by at her level, 100’s of feet above the crowds. Gift Boxes, the size of houses, followed. She could see dear old Santa at the end, rounding the corner. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">A new element appeared. A young man, holding a remote control. He and his father were wearing Santa hats, and fake white beards. She followed their gaze toward a small Unmanned Arial Vehicle. It was shaped like the Millennium falcon, but she could see cameras attached to it. They were apparently trying to get a better glimpse of the parade. It flew in an out of the balloons. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">That’s when she noticed that they seemed to be avoiding the television cameras, and security stations. Quite expertly in fact. It was odd, these visions, she could see near and far at the same time without obscuring either set of images. The pair were not smiling, like the rest of the crowd. They were not pointing and laughing at the parade floats. The father was whispering in the son’s ear, and the son was intent on following the path of his UAV. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">The toy plane made its way up and over the last set of balloons, and then down toward Santa, nearly knocking him off his pedestal. He looked more annoyed than frightened. It swooped back up to the last balloon, and under.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta willed herself to float down for a better look. As it approached the underside, a small package fixed to the vinyl started to emit a red light. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Wait… no. The red light wasn’t coming from the package, it was coming from somewhere else. The UAV. A metal point was on the front end of the UAV and attached to it was a laser pointer. The kind you might find at Dollar Tree. It was flying straight up now, 90 degrees, at full speed. The red dot fixed directly at the middle of the box.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Impact. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She’d come to expect what was next. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">First the flash…<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">…then the percussion against her chest…<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">…then the sound of explosion.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She was hurled through the sky half way across the city. The devastation was enormous. Whole buildings collapsed. People that had not been crushed were running from the scene. But it would do no good. The air was windy, and an orange cloud wafted in all directions. The runners were dropping dead within feet, some within blocks.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Walking, nearly oblivious to the commotion, through the dust were a father and son. Wearing gas masks. They hopped onto a parked motorcycle, on a side street. And headed into the city, and they were gone. She could just make the first three numbers of the Virginia License, K38…<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta could see the kid smile, raise his fingers to the sky, and toss the Santa hat and beard to the wind. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She watched as the costume floated through the air, landing on a body nearby.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">***<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta woke. The dream, night after night. She lay in her bed, staring at the ceiling for a few moments. Knowing that sleep would not soon return, she got up and walked to the window. She opened it and let the cool air rush over her. It almost reminded her of home.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She couldn’t get the image of Santa out of her head. She always thought that a white Santa was odd. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Everyone knows that Santa was a dark skinned Russian.” Her Mamochka would start the heated, good natured debate with Da’. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“No dear,” Da’ would counter. “He was an Irishman, plain as the nose on your face.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">And on the debate would run. Reindeer? Please. Not these brown skinny things in American cartoons. He used large chested white Reindeer, with long pointed antlers, like tree branches with no leaves. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">That’s the Santa her Mamochka taught her about.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Of course, there is no Santa… no elves, no fairies, no Jesus, no Mohamed, no Buddha. All trappings of religious non-sense to keep ignorant people in line, and full of pointless hope. And when those ignorant people got too far out of line, they sent in Eta to put them down. Well… they used to, until she got broken. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“So…” She broke the silence. Considering the conversation earlier that night. “I’m not just broken… I’m also a failed experiment. Why doesn’t that surprise me?” Her voice fell deaf on the evening breeze.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Images of her parents, when they were alive. Christmas with family and friends. Like it should be. Presents, not many, too poor. But they would come up with something every Christmas, despite themselves. And then her life changed. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“America?” She said one day. “Really? We’re going to America?” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">All she’d ever known was the temperate town of Letterkenny, Ireland. Her mother fled to Ireland during World War 2, when the Russians were working loosely with the westerners. She got her chance to leave as a “Volunteer Field Nurse”. She managed to claim asylum in newly formed Republic of Ireland, who had been sitting out the war as a neutral party. The Irish were sympathetic to her cause, as they too were under pressure constant pressure to roll over and accept the so called “United Kingdom”. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">That’s where she met her Irish lover, a man after her own heart. They thought that kids were not in their cards, until she got pregnant in her mid 40’s. Her parents were older than the other kid’s parents, but she didn’t mind. It made her special. At least, that’s what she was told growing up.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">At 10 years old, in 1976, her father, frightened by the escalating violence and the Kingsmill Massacre, decided to leave. He took his wife and daughter to start over in America. That’s when it all fell apart. He worked two jobs, with no family to fall back on, and died of a heart attack before she turned 16. Her mother died a year later of pneumonia, probably compounded by grief. By 18 she was the ridicule of school with the oddest of odd accents, and she didn’t fit anywhere. Then she was left to fend for herself. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Not to worry,” a friend told her. “The military takes freaks like you all the time.”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">And with a 99 on the ASVAB, she could do anything she wanted. She found a job in intelligence, and worked her way up the ranks. Which led her to today… a total mess.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">Eta brushed off the past, and tried to remember the new elements of her dream.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She could still see the face of the young man with the remote, the father not so much.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">One word popped into her head. It sounded so funny. She said it out loud. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">“Waqas”<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She rolled it onto her tongue, and repeated it over and over. She found the word instantly amusing, but sad at the same time.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">She let the images, of the dream and her childhood Christmas, battle each other for another hour, looking out the window into the night sky. Eventually she felt a yawn come on, stretching the back of her throat. That was a good sign that the dream was wearing off, and she could head back to bed. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">The covers swallowed her, like an ocean. And she was the island swallowed by it.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Discovery</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Ash and Eta compare the files on dark matter to the dark net search results.<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Dr. Briar needs to tie the boston bombings to a series of information security breaches. And point him toward Dark Matter.<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">They discover information on Dark Matter, the boston bombings, and the pattern search algoruhtym points them toward a particular terrorist cell in the north east. <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">They were gathered around the multi-monitor set of screens, and Chester displayed the most likely candidates for further review. </span><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Closet to Rooftop</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Back at Chesters, Ash is resting in the bedroom, when is transported to a man on a roof top, then ends up back in his bedroom.
