What he learns gives them their next clue. A Terrorist named ..... doesn't want to go through with it. They must find him.<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">It was the sound. It washed over his mind, like a shower on the inside. Water landing on pavement, concrete, and wood; as though beating a million tiny drums. The distant sound of the tiny river, washing down the sidewalks into the gutters. Ash always slept with the window open, but most especially on rainy nights.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">We are no closer now to getting to the bottom of this, than we were when it started.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The rest of the house was asleep, but he couldn't find sleep. The air shifted slightly, and he felt he wasn't alone. Ash sat up straight, and at the edge of his bed sat a man. He was wearing blue jeans, a T-shirt, and a well-kept beard. His hair was pulled back into a pony tail, and he smiled. The man's eyes held a piercing gaze, frightening and peaceful all at once.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The man stood, and motioned to Ash. Almost unable to fight his own body, he rose to follow the man to the closet door. He reached out to open it, and a blinding light raced across the room. When Ash opened his eyes again, they were not in his room anymore. They were standing on a rooftop, overlooking New York City, and Herald Square.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">There, sitting on the ledge was a young man, maybe no more than 20. He was holding himself, rocking himself back and forth.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">It'll all be over soon. It'll all be over soon. He repeated to himself over and over.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash could see the Parade balloons in the distance. He knew this is what Eta had been talking about. Ash wanted to go stop him, but he couldn't move. His traveling companion put his arm on Ash's shoulder and smiled. He could feel the answer.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Not Now.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The bright light flashed, and he found himself standing in front of his open closet, staring at the darkness. Ash turned back to his bed, and lay there for a few more hours. Sleep overcame him, and he rested. For the first time in a long time, confused, but peaceful, he rested.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">First Macy's Vision</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; She's standing in midair, 100 feet or more above the crowds. She can see a line of people in costumes dancing down the street, with large decorated vehicles in between groups. There are crowds on both sides of the street bundled in heavy clothing and a light dusting of snow from earlier today. She doesn't feel the chill, she's a spectator, not a participant. Eta floats, stationary, observing.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Suddenly, her vision is blocked by something brown and right up in her face, she wills herself a few steps back, and a large brown balloon fills her vision. A few more steps back and it's as large as a house. As it passes she can take in a full view. It's a... turkey? Yes, it's a turkey the size of a house, floating high above the crowds, and just below and even with her.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; She can see Santa on his sleigh toward the end of the event... She knows this somehow. It's familiar, something her parents were involved in before she shut out them, their memories, and society in general. What's it called? There's a tradition for this? Santa and his float are just below her now. She sees the clock on the building, it's 1:17 pm...<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Flash. Her chest is compressed, she can't breathe. The buildings around her crumble. She is thrown back 1,000's of feet. She takes the next breath she sees the cloud, just like old WWII videos. A mushroom cloud. The buildings are evaporating in every direction. The inhabitants won't even know what hit them until it's far, far too late. It's moving with great speed for miles, buildings evaporate before the wave in every direction.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Devastation...<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">As she sucks in a deep breath the world around her evaporates and turns black, then white, then she wakes up. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">***<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Ash was white. "You do know what you are saying right?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"I guess so. Some parade is about to be attacked?" <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">"Not just some parade, Eta. The Macy's Thanksgiving Day Parade! That's an icon of Americana. It's filled with innocent people, including lots of children. That would be a disaster beyond the Twin Towers." Ash sat on a stool and sighed. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">New Scene</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;"><br clear="all" style="mso-special-character: line-break; page-break-before: always;" /></span> <br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 16.0pt;">Episode 3 - Checkmate</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">New Scene 2</span></b><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 16.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Your Move</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Dark Matter escapes. But his base of operations is shut down for now. Dr. Briar is running for his life from the Anonymous Benefactor. <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Like Moriarty to Sherlock, Darkmatter is playing a long term game. He has his players separated from each other not just in location, but in construct. His Jihadists don’t' know about his drug sales, etc. He has False ID's upon False ID's. Layers of organization. He currently works out of a hotel that he owns under a shell Corp. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The F.B.I., working with Ash, raid a hotel, arresting over 100 people. Computers, hard drives, disks, records, all confiscated. The best stuff is on a single computer upstairs; but the disk was wiped clean. Darkmatter is smart, but he's no hacker. The data is corrupted, but not totally lost. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">In the corner of the room, Ash finds a chessboard, with one pawn knocked over. There is a word written on the side of the white piece. Boom! <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Ash's phone rings; and Darkmatter says: "let the games begin... Nice hat by the way. You should get that stain on your collar to the dry cleaner." Ash runs to the window, but sees nothing. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Post Credits</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: Dr. Briar leaves them with a list of other people affected by the experiments. Some of them are known to be dangerous, others have no apparent effect, and others may be struggling like they were. New Story Goal: ????<o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;"><br clear="all" style="mso-special-character: line-break; page-break-before: always;" /></span> <br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: center;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 16.0pt;">Un-Used Scenes</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Change this to Ash's POV, and do something with it</span></b><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 16.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></b></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Randy Elwood - Sr Audio-Medical Equipment Technician. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">“Goooood… Did you see that? His blood pressure spiked and he stood up. Eye dilated. Something is starting. We’re almost there…” The voice said. Technician Randy Elwood took note of the time and date. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">***<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Randy Elwood. Sr Medical Equipment Technician at Briar Psychiatric Center. Randy has three PHD’s. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; • Audio Physics (Science of Sound) - Just because he could never stop dissecting music. Thought it would be fun to study sound. Turned out to be his favorite degree. Though, he wasn’t sure what he could do with it, other than make great You Tube Video Soundtracks, which is how he paid for most of his training and school. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; • One in Group Behavior - Maybe he could figure out how to interact with these humans he lived with. He’d never been much good throughout his life. Turns out the degree only made him better able to understand and predict human behavior, not mimic it to any realist believable degree. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; • Human Anatomy-Biology - Maybe understanding the body better he could understand the mind and social patterns. Not so much. Just made for more awkward conversations are the few parties he tried to attend. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Although incredibly bright, Randy was a perpetual student. His professors advised he leave the comforts of school for the real world. Only so much can be learned in the library. With a few phone calls he was connected to to the Briar Psychiatric Center to assist in the on going study of sound waves on the human mind.&nbsp;&nbsp; <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">He didn’t mind the gig. He spent most of his time in a lab, by himself, reading and watching monitors. Not bad for $100,000 a year job. Especially not bad for the first paying job he’d had outside of school. Real job anyway, he still made a few sound tracks now and then. This was a great gig for someone with his specialty: “Not interacting with other people.” <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Randy checked the monitors one last time before returning to his book. It was assigned reading by his new employer. Supposed to help the workers understand the “culture” of the company. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">“Unlocking the Paranormal Mind. A study of how the human mind’s unlocked potential”, By Dr Richard T. Briar. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Couldn’t be worse than doing nothing, or that whole semester he took wasted reading about the breeding behavior of wombats! <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">*** <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Boston Chaos</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The moonless midnight clung to the walls like a gulf oil spill. The flashes of color and bursts of brightness, reflected off of his glasses. Images of people screaming and running from the bombs. No one even noticed the purses and wallets being snatched. And no one noticed the three banks being hit. But most importantly, no one noticed the override being installed on the network cables underground a few blocks over. Lights on his computers spun to life. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Darkmatter, as he was known these days, got out of his chair and hopped around the room, laughing <i>hysterically</i>! <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"These idiots fall for the Jihad thing every time! It's like a broken record stuck on my favorite song, it never gets old." Darkmatter smiled to his latest conquests. The twins, fraternal brother and sister, were his biggest fans. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 105%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 105%;">His fingers went to blasting through the data now streaming into his network. He could see everything. From corporate servers, to granny's desktops. He had access to it all. And they wouldn't notice for months! They'll be too busy looking for some rag tag brothers with a death wish. With the install re-buried, under the concrete, it will take a long time for anyone to figure out it's even there, if they ever do. In that amount of time, he'll have data mined millions of computers and servers. He can use this to install a botnet on every user connected to this line, and rotate out from there. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">All it cost was sending some lousy people off the planet early. They weren't doing anything productive anyway. His guides were proud of him, he could tell. They didn't say much, they just stood in the corner. But they were smiling. So he was on the right track. Too bad other's couldn't see Hecate and Adrasteia. Then again, why share their ancient knowledge with mere mortals. He was transcendant. One of the few on earth meant to ascend to higher realms of knowledge and power. The mortals would be gone eventually, so what if he sent a few early. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">He enjoyed the chaos that came working with the Jihadists. They were self destructive idiots, but in them he got a two for the price of one deal. He could cause the chaos needed for his empire to run, and take them out of the genetic pool at the same time. Win-Win.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The monitor on the wall buzzed, it was that Dr. Briar again. He was alerted when he was trying to be found online. He set up a program to alert him when it happened, and then send a false lead. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Brilliant." he said. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Excuse me sir." A large security gaurd stood in the doorway. "The fans are chanting for you." <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"We mustn't dissapoint the fans. 'Francis, we mustn' keep our fans waiting!' " A young man that could be his brother, if not for the Maryln Manson get up, nodded, and rushed out the door to the party downstairs. It's amazing how much noise 100's of teenagers and music can make, enough to cover up the real operations here. The key to operating an organization like this, it to ensure that you were not recognized as the man in charge. Set up a dummy, like Francis, to take the fall when it goes south. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">It will always go south, make contingencies. All the people who turn states evidence will sware that Francis was the king of this castle. And Walter Vanderbuilt, Darkmatter, Bob... Whatever name he was going by these days... Will just walk out the side door and dissappear. That's the beauty of this operation. If it wasn't for that silly Dr. Briar... He'd have no thorns at all.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Darkmatter lay on the couch, Hecate and Adrasteia came to him, massaging his shoulders, and whispering promises of the grand future. He closed his eyes and the darkness over took him... sweet comforting darkness. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">The Lair</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">As the tablet's glow grew to life, an image appeared. On the image of a full moon, two torches crossed paths at the top, over the scales of justice below. The torches were made of gold, but held black and white flames. Upon the gold scales sat two weights named Adrasteia and Hekate . <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Dark Elf entered his password, and the screens around the room booted to life. As he checked his monitors he was able to observe 1000's of cameras from around the world, but front and center were his prime monitors. From here he could see his entire operation. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Anonymous served it's purpose, now it was time to come out of the shadows. It was amazing how much could be accomplished through some keystrokes and well placed religious bull. Dark Elf roamed the dark net, smirking periodically at Hacker Aliases like "HellRazzer567". Really? That's the best you could come up with. Children. It was time to establish his own ID. Something to set him apart from his Anonymous friends. Dark Elf would do nicely. It was simple enough to remember, innocuous enough to be tracked to 100's of other individuals. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">So far the instructions of his masters had paid dividends many times over. He was rich, powerful, and operated from the shadows. Just levitate a few chairs and the powerless will flock to you as though you had all the answers. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">The Islamist Extremists were so easy to manipulate too. It was astonishing how easy it was to get some bearded scarf wearer to bomb a building by hacking their website and posting the challenge. These people were not any smarter than these looser addicts that were constantly doing his bidding at his hotel. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">The great Dr. Briar was becoming a nuisance, but he was assured that he still served a purpose. He had a few ideas about what to do when that purpose was complete. Hiring a man to play the part of wealthy contributor? 100,000 dollars. The look on his face when he got the texts? Priceless. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">What a moron.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">Meanwhile, it was nice to be king. Lord rather. Lord Dark Elf. It had a nice ring to it, eh? <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">The best part of living in a hotel was total freedom. It was even better that he owned the hotel, and all the staff were his servants. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%; margin-bottom: 1.0pt; mso-layout-grid-align: none; mso-pagination: none; text-align: justify; text-autospace: none; text-indent: 4.0pt; text-justify: inter-ideograph;"><span lang="EN" style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt; line-height: 115%;">The room was dark, he'd been floating so many aliases, that <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><b><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Alternate</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">************<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Ash..." Chester spoke into the laptop mic. No response. He was still out cold. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The five entered the room&nbsp; with some fan fare. The lab coat turned to face the camera. It was Dr. Briar.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">&nbsp;"Hello... who ever you are." Dr. Briar smiled.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Chester and Eta looked at each other in horror.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"We've discovered your little virus. And we're quite safe here, I assure you." Dr. Briar turned to Ash and pulled out a siringe. He injected a clear solution directly into his neck. No reaction. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Dr. Briar turned to a computer system on the wall, and pushed a button, and winked at the camera. The monitor went blank. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta and Chester looked at each other, mouths open, a look fear crossed Chester's eyes. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Eta felt a sinking feeling in her stomach. She said nothing, she walked out the door, and started punching the pillar of the patio. <o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; font-size: 12.0pt;">Chester and Ms. Wu's</span></b><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><i><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;">Desc: <o:p></o:p></span></i></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><br /></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">They're here!<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Chester snapped awake...<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">His heart pumped against his chest like a battering ram, with the police just outside the door. Chester's skin was cold, clammy. Bed clothes were soaked.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Fog slowly cleared from his mind, under the light of a new dawn...<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">***asdf<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"They felt so real." Chester said to Ms. Wu, as he helped her run his own personal brand of Anti Virus software on the Orphanage Computers. He could have done it from home, but he enjoyed their weekly talks. "Black leather and mist were swirling about me, chasing me through the streets. I bolted into this old church on my right and locked the bolt. They were banging into the door, clambering to get through, the door was creaking... then I woke up."<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Interesting..." Ms. Wu said, as she picked up a "Coding Basics for Dummies" book, and placed it on the shelf.<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"It was so real... even though I've been awake for a few hours now, it still feels real."<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Let me ask.." turning to him, "Why were you afraid of them?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">The question had not occurred to him. "I...Why wouldn't I be? They were scary monsters."<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Sure, but they were locked outside, you were safe inside. You escaped them."<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"But there were getting in."<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">"Were they? Or were they trying to get in an failing?"<o:p></o:p></span></div><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt; text-align: justify; text-indent: 4pt;"><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 12pt;">Mr. Wu had a knack for turning things upside down.</span><span style="font-family: &quot;Arial&quot;,sans-serif; font-size: 12.0pt;"><o:p></o:p></span></div><br /><div class="MsoNormal" style="margin-bottom: 0.0001pt;"><br /></div>
